pi_items.source: Downloaded AuPbSn40 image from https://wsr.imagej.net/images/AuPbSn40.jpg (600 pixels wide). HFW = 256 µm (stated in microscope_log.txt). Correct calibration = 256 µm / 600 px = 0.427 µm/px. Metadata value 0.32 is incorrect as it assumes 800 pixels width.
Task description explicitly states 'measure the width (longest dimension) of at least 10 distinct bright grains'

Reasonable based on literature on Pb-Sn solder microstructure. For 600-pixel image with HFW 256 µm: 2 µm = 4.7 pixels (within visible range). Consistent with actual SEM grain sizes in solder alloys reported in research.

Reasonable range for SEM micrograph of multi-phase solder alloy. For 600-pixel image: 150 µm = 351 pixels (slightly exceeds image width). Consistent with microstructural variation in Au-Pb-Sn systems.
Task description explicitly states 'Settings: Width = 50 µm' for the scale bar in Analyze > Tools > Scale Bar

Downloaded actual AuPbSn40 image from https://wsr.imagej.net/images/AuPbSn40.jpg and verified dimensions using PIL Image library. Image is 600x412 pixels, not 800 pixels as claimed in task description and setup_task.sh.
Downloaded actual AuPbSn40 image and verified dimensions. Verifier.py claims 800x546, but actual image is 600x412 pixels.
Found working URL by downloading both mentioned sources. https://imagej.nih.gov/ij/images/AuPbSn40.jpg returns 404. Only https://wsr.imagej.net/images/AuPbSn40.jpg is accessible.
Stated in microscope_log.txt file created by setup_task.sh: 'Horizontal Field Width (HFW): 256 µm'

privileged_info_summary: The AuPbSn40 SEM micrograph is real data from the ImageJ sample repository (https://wsr.imagej.net/images/AuPbSn40.jpg), not synthetic. The actual image dimensions are 600x412 pixels, contradicting task claims of 800x546 pixels. This causes the calibration value to be incorrect: task metadata claims 0.32 µm/px (which assumes 800 pixels), but the correct value for the actual 600-pixel image with 256 µm HFW is 0.427 µm/px. The expected grain size range (2.0-150.0 µm) and scale bar size (50 µm) are reasonable and verified as consistent with the task description. Minimum measurement count (10 grains) is verified from task description.
